content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 085 — **two substantive prover lanes** continuing iter-084's structural advances; no subagent dispatch this iter per user policy 2026-05-11 "helpers go in the prover's own session" + "no thin scaffolding").
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill — C0 substantially complete with `instIsMonoidal_W` deferred, C1 scheduled iter-086+, C2 iter-087+, C3 iter-088+) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-084 ran two lanes; **Lane 2 landed a substantive structural closure** (`h_epi` of `cotangentExactSeq_structure` closed via Option (c)). Lane 1 made structural progress without closure. Net 14 → 14. Highlights:
20	
21	- **Lane 1 BasicOpenCech (structural advance only).** Both iter-083 typeclass barriers were cleared at L1334–1347: (1) the HSMul source-type ascription barrier is resolved by binding the R-module instance under the literal `↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` form (`letI hmod_pi_Z₁/Z₂`), not just `↑scK₀.X_i`; (2) the smul-commutation `e₁.symm (r • y) = r • e₁.symm y` is resolved by an explicit one-step `show ... rw [LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]` (the `Equiv.smul_def` collapse becomes a one-line rewrite once the e₁ ∘ e₁.symm reduction is visible). After this prelude, BOTH sides of the goal at L1383 have the clean form `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) <argument>)`; the residual is **genuine per-summand R-linearity content** (~50–80 LOC; S6–S8 chain from the iter-081/082 in-code documentation). Goal-side recipe: distribute through `Finset.sum_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` (perI₁) + `RingHom.map_mul` + `← presheaf.map_comp` + `algebraMap_naturality`; reassemble with `Finset.smul_sum` + `Finset.sum_congr rfl`.
22	
23	- **Lane 2 Differentials (`h_epi` CLOSED via Option (c)).** The conjunction `h_exact ∧ h_epi` was split via `refine ⟨?h_exact, ?h_epi⟩`; `h_epi` is now fully proved (L641–677) via `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` + `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` + `Submodule.span_induction` over `_root_.KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation`. The bundled-vs-unbundled typeclass bridge is sidestepped entirely by deriving the membership claim `hy := hspan ▸ (Submodule.mem_top : y ∈ ⊤)` implicitly (letting elaboration infer the `Module` instance type from the substitution rather than asserting an explicit type). Four span-induction cases (`mem`, `zero`, `add`, `smul`): `mem` closes via `unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta; exact ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d _ _`; the others via `map_zero`/`map_add`/`map_smul` + trailing `rfl`. The descent's behaviour on generators is given by `Universal.fac` (`desc d_target . d b = d_target . d b`). **`h_exact` remains as the single explicit sorry at L640**, deferred to this iter (iter-085).
24	
25	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
26	
27	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-085 plan |
28	|---|---|---|---|---|
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 1: close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1383) via the S6–S8 chain** atop iter-084's cleared typeclass prelude — distribute per-summand R-linearity through `Finset.sum_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` (perI₁) + `RingHom.map_mul` + `← presheaf.map_comp`. |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 5 | yes | B | **Lane 2: close `h_exact` of `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L640)** via the project-local helper `_root_.SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` (introduced **within the prover's own session** with `sorry` body permitted; the fifth permitted helper for this lemma, blueprint label `lem:sheafOfModules_exact_iff_stalkwise`) + Mathlib's `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` for the stalk-level reduction. |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 1 | yes | C0 | off-limits (deferred pending Mathlib upstream — `stalk_tensorObj` for varying-ring R₀) |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-088+) |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
34	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
35	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
36	| **Total syntactic** | **14** | — | | |
37	
38	## Current Objectives
39	
40	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
41	
42	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` via the S6–S8 chain
43	
44	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1383 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target — typeclass prelude landed iter-084), L1428 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1457 `h_loc_exact`.
45	
46	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1383). Iter-084 cleared both typeclass barriers (the two `letI hmod_pi_Z₁/Z₂` at L1334–1335 + the smul-commutation `rw` at L1344–1347). The remaining work is **genuine per-summand R-linearity** of the function `Φ : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) → ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂)`, `Φ z := (eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) z`, evaluated at the j-component. The recipe is the S6–S8 chain already documented in the in-code comment block above the sorry.
47	
48	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** These commits are essential prerequisites:
49	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418 (iter-078).
50	- `intro r y; funext j` at L1102–1103 (iter-078).
51	- The named-per-i `letI` block at L920–949 (iter-080): `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i) := …; letI h_mod_pi_n : Module R (∀ i, Z_n i) := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3.
52	- **The iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain at L1102–1153**: `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`; `have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n`; 5-layer `dsimp only`; full `simp [..., dif_pos hRel]`.
53	- **The iter-082 S5 prelude at L1161–1170**: `rw [show ... = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂ from rfl, show ... = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁ from rfl]` re-fold + `rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply, ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply]`. After this prelude, the `j`-projection is adjacent to the comp.
54	- **The iter-083 `letI := h_mod_X₁; letI := h_mod_X₂` block at L1207–1208**.
55	- **The iter-083 ~80-LOC findings comment block at L1198–1280**.
56	- **The iter-084 typeclass prelude at L1325–1382**: `letI hmod_pi_Z₁/Z₂` + smul-commutation `rw`. Documented findings: (1) HSMul barrier resolved by binding at `↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` form; (2) smul-commutation NOT rfl — needs explicit `LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply` rewrite. **Preserve this prelude byte-for-byte** as it is the gateway to the S6–S8 chain.
57	
58	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences. The R-action on `∀ i, Z_n i` is defined componentwise via `Pi.module`, where each `Z_n i = Γ(C.left, V_i)` is an R-algebra via the restriction map. The per-summand R-linearity at the j-component reduces to: `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op).hom` composed with `(presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r` equals `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r` (`← presheaf.map_comp`); and a ring-hom factoring of the product via `RingHom.map_mul`.
59	
60	**Recipe (S6–S8 chain, documented in-code at L1360–1378).**
61	
62	After the iter-084 typeclass prelude, the goal has the form
63	```
64	(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) (r • e₁.symm y))
65	  = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) (e₁.symm y))
66	```
67	where `Σ.hom = ∑ i, (-1)^i • Pi.lift ((Pi.π Z₁ (· ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i).op))`. The S6–S8 closure chain:
68	
69	- **S6 — distribute the j-projection and the smul through the eqToHom + Pi.lift + alternating sum.**
70	  - Apply `LinearMap.coe_comp` + `Function.comp_apply` to split the comp.
71	  - Apply `Finset.sum_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` (with `perI₁`) to distribute the sum across smul. At the j-component, this surfaces:
72	    ```
73	    Σ_i (-1)^i * (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i).op . hom ((r • e₁.symm y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))
74	      = r • Σ_i (-1)^i * (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i).op . hom (e₁.symm y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))
75	    ```
76	  - `Pi.lift_π` + `(Pi.π Z₁ k) ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv z) = z k` (use `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` or directly the `Pi.lift_π` reduction).
77	- **S7 — per-summand R-linearity at fixed `i`.** The j-component of the i-th summand reduces to:
78	  ```
79	  (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op).hom ((r • z) (j ∘ δ_i))
80	    = (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op).hom
81	        ((presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i a0} ≤ U).op).hom r * z (j ∘ δ_i))
82	  ```
83	  by `Pi.smul_apply` and the `perI₁` definition. Apply `RingHom.map_mul` to split the product; then apply `← C.left.presheaf.map_comp` to collapse the algebra-map chain `R = Γ(U) → Γ(V_{j∘δ_i a0}) → Γ(V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(V_j)` to `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r`. This matches the `perI₂ j` definition of `r •_{Z₂ j} _`. The decisive lemma is project-local `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality` (`Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161) which states `(toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ).op.hom (algebraMap R _ r) = algebraMap R _ r` — i.e., the restriction is an R-algebra hom.
84	- **S8 — reassemble.** `Finset.smul_sum` on the RHS distributes `r •` across the sum; `Finset.sum_congr rfl` with the per-summand identity from S7 closes the goal.
85	
86	**Partial-credit fallback.** If S7 stalls on the `RingHom.map_mul` collapse or the `algebraMap_naturality` invocation, split out an intermediate `have` lemma at the per-summand level and document precisely which step failed. Even with one named `have` stalled at `?_`, the S6 + S8 wrapping is **substantial iter-085 advance**.
87	
88	**Approaches confirmed dead-end iter-084** (do NOT re-attempt):
89	- `letI : Module ↑R ↑scK₀.X₁ := h_mod_X₁` (typed at `↑scK₀.X_i`) without the additional `letI : Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` — insufficient for the HSMul synthesis when the outer source is `↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` (the two types are defeq via `dsimp`, but typeclass search is syntactic).
90	- `simp [Equiv.smul_def]` on the smul commutation — does NOT fire (`simp` argument unused). Use the explicit `show ... rw [LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]` instead.
91	- Naive `rw [map_smul]` on the LHS — fails at the `MulActionHomClass` synthesis (e₁ is `≃ₗ[k]`, not `≃ₗ[R]`).
92

150	
151	**Mathematical content.**
152	- The stalkwise criterion: a short complex of sheaves of $\mathcal R$-modules is exact iff its image under each stalk functor $(-)_x$ is exact in the corresponding category of modules at $x$.
153	- Per-stalk reduction: at $x \in X$, the cotangent short complex $f^*\Omega_{Y/S} \to \Omega_{X/S} \to \Omega_{X/Y}$ has stalk $\Omega_{B/A} \otimes_{B'} B \to \Omega_{C/A} \to \Omega_{C/B} \to 0$ where $A \to B \to C$ are the local rings at $f(x), g(x), x$ respectively — exactness is Mathlib's `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`.
154	
155	**Recipe.**
156	
157	**Step 1 — Introduce the helper at top level** (above `cotangentExactSeq_structure`, after the existing four helpers; the prover writes it inside its own session):
158	```lean
159	/-- The stalkwise criterion for exactness of a short complex of sheaves of modules:
160	    a short complex is exact iff its image under each stalk functor is exact in the
161	    corresponding category of modules at that point. Mathlib-gap-fill. -/
162	lemma _root_.SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise
163	    {C : Type*} [Category C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
164	    {R : Sheaf J RingCat} (S : ShortComplex (SheafOfModules R)) :
165	    S.Exact ↔ ∀ x, ((S.map (SheafOfModules.stalkFunctor R x)).Exact) := by
166	  -- Mathlib gap: requires the SheafOfModules stalk functor at each topos point
167	  -- (`SheafOfModules.stalkFunctor`), which bridges `TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor`
168	  -- (currently AddCommGrp-level) to `SheafOfModules R`. Reserved for future Mathlib
169	  -- upstream PR or a multi-iteration project-local gap-fill.
170	  sorry
171	```
172	NOTE: The exact signature above is illustrative — the actual signature must use the project's existing `SheafOfModules` framework and the `relativeDifferentials` setup. Use `lean_hover_info` and `lean_local_search` to identify the correct `stalkFunctor` name and `Exact` formulation. The signature **must** be such that it directly applies to the `cotangentShortComplex` in `h_exact`'s context.
173	
174	**Step 2 — Replace the `case h_exact => sorry` body at L640** with:
175	```lean
176	case h_exact =>
177	  rw [SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise]
178	  intro x
179	  -- Per-stalk reduction: at each x ∈ X, the cotangent short complex's stalk
180	  -- is f^*Ω_{Y/S} ⊗_{O_X} k(x) → Ω_{X/S} ⊗_{O_X} k(x) → Ω_{X/Y} ⊗_{O_X} k(x), which
181	  -- by definitions reduces to Ω_{B/A} ⊗_{B'} B → Ω_{C/A} → Ω_{C/B} (the ring-level
182	  -- KaehlerDifferential exactness chain at the local rings at x).
183	  -- Apply `_root_.KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` after the
184	  -- stalk-of-relativeDifferentials identification.
185	  exact _root_.KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map ... -- fill exact args
186	```

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy

69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems

37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).

70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1

419	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
420	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
421	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
422	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
423	schemes in our setting.
424	
425	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
426	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
427	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
428	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
429	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
430	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
431	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
432	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
433	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
434	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
435	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
436	
437	The structural skeleton is:
438	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
439	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
440	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
441	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
442	
443	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration

445	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
446	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
447	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
448	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
449	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
450	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
451	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
452	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
453	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
454	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
455	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
456	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
457	
458	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of

## Found Signatures

1. **`ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Products`]
   `(Z : ι → ModuleCat R) (i : ι) : (piIsoPi Z).inv ≫ Pi.π Z i = ModuleCat.ofHom (LinearMap.proj i)`
   - Element form: **`ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι_apply`**: `(ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z i)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (piIsoPi Z).inv) x) = x i`

2. **`ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Products`]
   `(Z : ι → ModuleCat R) (i : ι) : (piIsoPi Z).hom ≫ ModuleCat.ofHom (LinearMap.proj i) = Pi.π Z i`
   - Element form: **`ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply`**: `(ConcreteCategory.hom (piIsoPi Z).hom) x i = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z i)) x`

3. **`CategoryTheory.Limits.Pi.lift_π_apply`** [`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Products`]
   `(p : (b : β) → P ⟶ f b) (b : β) (x : carrier P) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π f b)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.lift p)) x) = (ConcreteCategory.hom (p b)) x`
   - (Concrete-category version; works directly for `ModuleCat`.)
   - Pure type-theoretic version: **`CategoryTheory.Limits.Types.pi_lift_π_apply`**: `Pi.π f b (Pi.lift s x) = s b x`

4. **`LinearMap.add_apply`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LinearMap.Defs`]
   `(f g : M →ₛₗ[σ₁₂] M₂) (x : M) : (f + g) x = f x + g x`
   **`Finset.sum_apply`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Pi`]
   `(a : α) (s : Finset ι) (g : ι → (a : α) → M a) : (∑ c ∈ s, g c) a = ∑ c ∈ s, g c a`

5. **`RingHom.map_mul`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.Ring.Hom.Defs`]
   `(f : α →+* β) (a b : α) : f (a * b) = f a * f b`
   - Generic version: **`map_mul`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Hom.Defs`]: `(f : F) (x y : M) : f (x * y) = f x * f y` (any `MulHomClass`)

6. **`CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp`** [`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.Basic`]
   `(F : Functor C D) (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) : F.map (f ≫ g) = F.map f ≫ F.map g`
   - Note: For an `Opens X`-presheaf, the contravariance in `Cᵒᵖ` automatically gives `presheaf.map (f ≫ g) = presheaf.map g ≫ presheaf.map f` in the original direction (this is just `Functor.map_comp` applied through `op`).
   - Topological-presheaf composition app form: **`TopCat.Presheaf.comp_app`**: `(f ≫ g).app U = f.app U ≫ g.app U`

   `(e : M ≃ₛₗ[σ] M₂) (b : M) : e.symm (e b) = b`

10. **`CategoryTheory.eqToHom_app`** [`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.EqToHom`]
    `{F G : Functor C D} (h : F = G) (X : C) : (eqToHom h).app X = eqToHom (by rw [h])`
    - For element-level via `ConcreteCategory`, combine with **`CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply`**: `(ConcreteCategory.hom (f ≫ g)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom g) ((ConcreteCategory.hom f) x)`
    - Useful related: **`CategoryTheory.Functor.congr_hom`** (transport along `F = G`).

11. **`ModuleCat.hom_zsmul`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic`]
    `(n : ℤ) (f : M ⟶ N) : ModuleCat.Hom.hom (n • f) = n • ModuleCat.Hom.hom f`

    `(a : S) (f : M →ₛₗ[σ₁₂] M₂) (x : M) : (a • f) x = a • f x`

12. **`ModuleCat.Hom.hom`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic`]
    `{A B : ModuleCat R} (f : A.Hom B) : ↑A →ₗ[R] ↑B` (the underlying `LinearMap`)
    - Bijection lemmas: `ModuleCat.hom_bijective`, `ModuleCat.hom_injective`, `ModuleCat.hom_surjective`, `ModuleCat.ofHom_hom`, `ModuleCat.hom_id`, `ModuleCat.hom_ext`, `ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff`.
    - For `eqToHom` to underlying coercion you generally use `eqToHom_app` then `cast`/`ModuleCat.hom_id`-style rewrites; no dedicated `eqToHom.hom_apply` lemma was found.

## Other useful discoveries

- **`CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply`**: `(ConcreteCategory.hom (f ≫ g)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom g) ((ConcreteCategory.hom f) x)` — useful for unfolding compositions on elements.
- **`CategoryTheory.Limits.limit.lift_π_apply`** [`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.Elementwise`]: ConcreteCategory version of `limit.lift ≫ limit.π = c.π.app j` applied to elements.
- **`CategoryTheory.Limits.Pi.lift_π`**: the bare equation `Pi.lift p ≫ Pi.π f b = p b` (no `_apply`).
- **`CategoryTheory.Functor.congr_hom`**: `F.map f = eqToHom h ≫ G.map f ≫ eqToHom h'` when `F = G` — useful for moving `eqToHom` around.
- **`Pi.smul_def`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.Notation.Pi.Defs`]: `a • f = fun i => a • f i` (function-form companion to `Pi.smul_apply`).
- **`ModuleCat.HasLimit.lift_hom_apply`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Biproducts`]: a ModuleCat-specific elementwise lift lemma — handy if working with `HasLimit.lift` directly.
- **`AddMonoidHom.finset_sum_apply`** [`Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Group.Finset.Lemmas`]: `(∑ x ∈ s, f x) b = ∑ x ∈ s, (f x) b` for `f : ι → M →+ N` — analog of `LinearMap` version.

        -- is `Pi.smul_apply`-fired by typeclass instance `[∀ i, SMul R (Z₁ i)]`).

        -- form (via the same `LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply` collapse used by iter-084).

            e₁.symm (r • y) := by
          show e₁.symm (r • e₁ (e₁.symm y)) = e₁.symm (r • y)
          rw [LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
        rw [hsmul_eq]
        -- ===========================================================================
        -- ITER-085 obstruction: HOU mismatch on `LinearMap.comp_apply`.
        -- ===========================================================================
        -- After the `rw [hsmul_eq]` above, the LHS reads
        --   `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom).hom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) (e₁.symm (r •_pi y))`
        -- and the RHS reads
        --   `r •_{perI₂ j} (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom).hom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) (e₁.symm y)`.
        -- The next step S6 — split the `∘ₗ`-comp via `LinearMap.comp_apply` — fails
        -- (verified via `lean_multi_attempt` iter-085) for the following reasons:
        --   (a) `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` does NOT fire — the lemma is stated
        --       for `∘ₛₗ` (semilinear comp) with explicit `RingHom.id` ring-hom, while
        --       the goal has the homogeneous `∘ₗ` notation; higher-order unification
        --       fails to bridge the implicit ring-hom triple.
        --   (b) `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]` (with or without `(σ₁₂ := RingHom.id k)`

        --       elaboration internally expands the `∘ₗ` to a `LinearMap.comp` call
        --       that is not pattern-matched by the lemma's HOU surface form.
        --   (c) `change ((eqToHom).hom (Σ.hom ...)) = ...` fails: the implicit

        --       inferred without the original elaborator context.
        --   (d) `induction hRel; rfl` fails with motive issue (n appears in many

        --       `h_mod_X_i`/`h_a₀`/`h_a₀_fun` etc.).

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean

## Iter-085 status

**Result:** IN PROGRESS — `h_diff_pi_smul_f` body NOT closed. Sorry count
unchanged: **6** (file compiles cleanly, 0 errors). Hard cap honored.

**Substantive structural progress (iter-085 advance):** added the `hsmul_eq`
rewrite (L1399-1402) that surfaces the inner Pi.module smul. The goal now
reads `(...) (e₁.symm (r •_pi y)) = r •_{perI₂ j} (...) (e₁.symm y)` instead
of the iter-084 form `(...) (r •_{h_mod_X₁} e₁.symm y) = ...`. This puts the
expression in the form S6-S8 wants for the per-summand reduction.

## h_diff_pi_smul_f (line ~1407, sorry now at line 1447)

### Iter-085 attempt — what was added

1. **`hsmul_eq` rewrite (L1399-1402):** introduced the identity
   `r • (piIsoPi Z₁).symm y = e₁.symm (r • y)` (with inner Pi.module smul) and
   `rw [hsmul_eq]`-applied it. Verified via `lean_goal` that the LHS now contains
   `e₁.symm (r • y)` (Pi.module smul) instead of `r • e₁.symm y` (transported).

2. **Comprehensive HOU obstruction documentation (L1410-1437):** the next
   step S6 — split `(eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) (r • z) = eqToHom (Σ.hom (r • z))` —
   was attempted via four routes, all failed:
   - `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` — argument unused (no fire).
   - `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]` — pattern `(?f ∘ₗ ?g) ?x` not found.
   - `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply (σ₁₂ := RingHom.id k) (σ₂₃ := RingHom.id k)]`
     with explicit ring-hom hints — pattern `?m ∘ₗ ?m'` not found in target.
   - `change ((eqToHom).hom (Σ.hom ...)) = ...` — `eqToHom` cast type proof
     cannot be inferred without the original elaborator context.
   - `induction hRel; rfl` — motive issue (n appears in many other hypotheses
     including `Z_i`, `e_i`, `perI_i`, `h_mod_pi_i`, `h_mod_X_i`, `h_a₀`, etc.).

3. **Iter-086 path forward outlined in code (L1428-1440):** construct an
   explicit per-summand R-linear restriction map as an inline `have`:
   ```
   have R_restrict_i : ∀ (i : Fin (prev n + 2)) (i_1 : ...) (z : Z₁ (i_1 ∘ δ_i)),
     (presheaf.map h₂.op).hom (perI₁ (i_1 ∘ δ_i)).smul r z =
       (perI₂ i_1).smul r ((presheaf.map h₂.op).hom z)
   ```
   Provable via `presheaf.map_comp` collapsing the algebra-map chain
   `R = Γ(U) → Γ(V_{i_1 a0}) → Γ(V_{i_1 ∘ δ_i}) → Γ(V_{i_1})`
   to `Γ(U) → Γ(V_{i_1 a0'}) → Γ(V_{i_1})` via `← presheaf.map_comp`.
   Then `Finset.sum_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` + `Pi.lift_π_apply` + `R_restrict_i`
   per-summand + `Finset.smul_sum` (S8 reassembly) closes.

### Approaches confirmed dead-end (iter-085)

- `simp only [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply, Finset.smul_sum, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, ModuleCat.hom_sum, ModuleCat.hom_zsmul, LinearMap.sum_apply, ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι_apply, CategoryTheory.Limits.Pi.lift_π_apply]` — **NONE of these 11 lemmas fire**. The `(eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) (...)` term is opaque to all standard rewriting because of the homogeneous `∘ₗ` notation and the ConcreteCategory.hom wrapping of the eqToHom and the sum.
- `set L : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) →ₗ[k] ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := ...` to bind the comp as a local LinearMap — fails with universe constraint stuck (`u =?= imax ?u' ?u''` for the Pi-type elaboration).
- `have key : ∀ z, (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (... z) = ?_` to bundle the j-projection of the comp as an inline universally-quantified hypothesis — fails because the eqToHom's inferred type proof and the `∑ i, ...` sum's element type cannot be unified with the underdetermined `?_`.

### Why this is structural progress despite still being a sorry

The iter-084 prelude established the typeclass scaffolding (the two `letI hmod_pi_Z_i` and the smul-commutation `rw`) so that the post-S5 goal could *type-check* with both sides referring to compatible `r •` operations. The iter-085 `hsmul_eq` rewrite + obstruction documentation:

1. **Cleanly identifies the residual mathematical content** — the goal is now `(comp) (e₁.symm (r •_pi y)) = r •_{perI₂ j} (comp) (e₁.symm y)`, which is "F is R-linear in r" where F = `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ∘ (eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) ∘ e₁.symm`. The R-action on source is `h_mod_pi₁` (Pi.module), and on target is `perI₂ j` (`RingHom.toModule`).

2. **Pinpoints the precise HOU obstruction** — the iter-085 documentation lists exactly which 5 standard rewriting tactics fail and why. This shifts the iter-086 plan from "attempt the S6 chain" to "construct the explicit per-summand R-linear restriction map and bypass the comp-application via `congr_arg`-style equational reasoning".

3. **Provides a concrete iter-086 recipe** — the per-summand `R_restrict_i` `have` with `presheaf.map_comp`-driven proof is a self-contained ~30 LOC inline `have` that, once landed, lets `Finset.sum_congr rfl`-reduce the goal to its closing form.

### Mathlib references discovered (verified iter-085, all exist)

- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι_apply` — `(Pi.π Z i).hom ((piIsoPi Z).inv x) = x i` — needed for S6 per-component reduction.
- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` — `(piIsoPi Z).hom x i = (Pi.π Z i).hom x` — already used by iter-082 S5 prelude.
- `CategoryTheory.Limits.Pi.lift_π_apply` — `(Pi.π f b).hom ((Pi.lift p).hom x) = (p b).hom x` — needed for S6 per-component reduction.
- `LinearMap.comp_apply` — `(f ∘ₛₗ g) x = f (g x)` — **STATED FOR `∘ₛₗ`** (semilinear); doesn't fire on goal's `∘ₗ` (homogeneous) form. KEY OBSTRUCTION.
- `Pi.smul_apply` — `(a • f) i = a • f i` — needed for S6/S7.
- `Finset.sum_apply`, `Finset.smul_sum`, `Finset.sum_congr` — needed for S8 reassembly.
- `RingHom.map_mul`, `← Functor.map_comp` (specifically `← C.left.presheaf.map_comp`) — needed for S7 per-summand collapse.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality` (project-local, `StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161) — for k-algebra hom; for R-algebra hom version, use `presheaf.map_comp` + uniqueness of morphisms in `Opens` directly.
- `ModuleCat.hom_sum`, `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul`, `LinearMap.smul_apply` — none fire on the current goal's `(eqToHom ∘ₗ ModuleCat.Hom.hom (∑ i, ...))` form because the `∑` is wrapped inside `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` of a categorical-sum.

### Constraints honored

- **Sorry count = 6** (no regression; hard cap honored).
- **Iter-080 `letI` refactor at L920-949** preserved byte-for-byte.
- **`set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418** preserved.
- **Iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain at L1102-1153** preserved byte-for-byte.
- **Iter-082 S5 prelude at L1161-1170** preserved byte-for-byte.
- **Iter-083 `letI := h_mod_X₁; letI := h_mod_X₂` block at L1207-1208** preserved byte-for-byte.
- **Iter-083 ~80 LOC findings comment block at L1198-1280** preserved byte-for-byte.
- **Iter-084 typeclass prelude at L1325-1382** preserved byte-for-byte.
- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (per user policy 2026-05-11).
- **No new axioms.**
- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation.** Used `lean_diagnostic_messages` (allowed) and `lean_multi_attempt` (allowed) to verify each substep.
- **Off-limits sorries (L502, L826, L854, L1481 [g_R.map_smul'], L1510 [h_loc_exact])** untouched.

## File state at end of iter-085

- `BasicOpenCech.lean`: 6 sorries (no regression). File compiles cleanly.
- `h_diff_pi_smul_f` body extended with iter-085 substantive structural advance:
  the `hsmul_eq` rewrite + 4 documented dead-end tactics + iter-086 path forward.
- All preserved iter-080-084 work intact byte-for-byte.

## Recommendations for plan agent (iter-086)

**Lane 1 (this file) iter-086 plan:** the iter-086 prover should construct the
per-summand R-linear restriction map `R_restrict_i` as an inline `have` BEFORE
the `rw [hsmul_eq]` at L1399. Specifically:

```lean
have R_restrict_R_linear : ∀ (V W : Opens C.left.toTopCat) (h_VW : V ≤ W)
    (h_VU : V ≤ U) (h_WU : W ≤ U) (r' : R) (z : C.left.presheaf.obj W.op),
    (C.left.presheaf.map h_VW.op).hom
      ((C.left.presheaf.map h_WU.op).hom r' * z) =
    (C.left.presheaf.map h_VU.op).hom r' *
      (C.left.presheaf.map h_VW.op).hom z := by
  intro V W h_VW h_VU h_WU r' z
  rw [(C.left.presheaf.map h_VW.op).hom.map_mul,
      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp,
      show ((h_WU.op : W.op ⟶ U.op) ≫ (h_VW.op : V.op ⟶ W.op)) =
        (h_VU.op : V.op ⟶ U.op) from rfl]  -- uniqueness of morphisms in Opens
```

This is provable in ~10 LOC and is the KEY for S7. After this, the iter-086
chain proceeds with `Finset.sum_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` + `Pi.lift_π_apply` +
`R_restrict_R_linear` (per-summand) + `Finset.smul_sum` (S8 reassembly). The
HOU mismatch on `LinearMap.comp_apply` from iter-085 can be bypassed by using
`congr_arg (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` to pull the j-projection inside, then leveraging
`(eqToHom ⋯).hom`'s identity-after-substituting-hRel character via `eqToHom_app`
or a focused `change`.

**Iter-086 estimated complexity:** ~50-80 LOC inline. The `R_restrict_R_linear`
helper is ~10 LOC; the per-summand reduction with reassembly is the bulk.

**Critical path note:** the iter-086 prover should NOT attempt to use
`LinearMap.comp_apply` directly on the current goal form. Instead, work
backwards: start with the closed-form RHS `r •_{perI₂ j} (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (...)`
and rewrite using `Finset.smul_sum` + per-summand `R_restrict_R_linear` + `←`
the same chain to match the LHS. This is the "S8 → S7 → S6" reverse direction.
